previous_issues_status.new_label: STRIKE 6 — expanded: coffee corner, weather label, DUSK window ALL confirmed escaping in screenshots

previous_issues_status.issue: STRIKE 3: Triple visibilitychange listeners — one fires unconditionally
NEW: completionToast timer ref clobbers itself on multi-worker ship
NEW: Sparkline legend pills use array index as React key
STRIKE 3: Utility functions not extracted to src/utils/
STRIKE 3: isDimmed incorrectly dims workers with no current task
STRIKE 5: Mobile office floor text remains illegible at 375px
STRIKE 4: Footer tagline pagination counter kills the magic
STRIKE 3: Office.css duplicate vibe selectors lines 91-95 dead code

suggestions.title: STRIKE 6 (EXPANDED): Coffee corner, DUSK window, and weather label all escape the office floor — confirmed in screenshots
suggestions.description: The office floor has overflow:visible, and at least 3 elements render visually outside the floor boundary at both desktop (1280px) and mobile (375px): (1) The coffee corner div with 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' label floats into the page background at the right; (2) The office window/DUSK sky element appears in the grey page background outside the floor; (3) The vibe weather label ('Clear skies — team at full strength') renders below and outside the floor boundary. This was originally the speech bubble escape bug — it has spread. Fix: audit all absolute-positioned Office elements with left/top > 85%. Either constrain overflow:hidden on the floor and use overflow:visible on a wrapper, or pull the escaping elements back within the floor bounds.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: handleVisibilityResume fires unconditional fetch on every tab-in, bypassing idle timeout
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 851: `const handleVisibilityResume = () => { if (!document.hidden) onResume() }`. This triggers a full syncFromGitHub() + clears pollingPaused on EVERY tab-in, regardless of whether the user was idle. The IDLE_TIMEOUT that suppresses polling is completely bypassed by tab switches. Tab away 2 seconds, come back: unconditional resync. Meanwhile the polling effect (line 869) ALSO has a visibilitychange listener that resets the poll interval. That's two visibilitychange handlers both firing on every tab-in, plus the title effect (line 1055) is a third. Fix the resume listener: gate it behind a minimum-away duration check before calling syncFromGitHub().

suggestions.title: STRIKE 7: Inline toast styles — completionToast and plantToast share 100+ lines of duplicated inline CSS
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1122-1145 (completionToast) and Office.jsx lines 1226-1249 (plantToast) each have massive inline style objects: position:fixed, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation — all inline and nearly identical. This is strike 7. Extract to a single .toast-popup CSS class in App.css. The two dynamic properties (border color and box-shadow tint based on worker role color) can stay as inline style overrides. Everything else is static.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 6: Activity filter radiogroup missing arrow-key navigation (WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1)
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1325-1364: two role=radiogroup containers, each with role=radio buttons. The ARIA radiogroup pattern requires arrow keys to move between options — Tab should jump over the group, not cycle through every button. Currently users Tab through every single filter option. Add onKeyDown to each radio button: ArrowRight/ArrowDown focus next sibling, ArrowLeft/ArrowUp focus previous, wrap around. Tab exits the group. This has been open since QA #46 — strike 6.

suggestions.title: NEW: polling-paused-pill aria-live is unreliable — live region must be in DOM from page load
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1173: the polling-paused-pill span has aria-live='polite', but it only mounts when pollingPaused becomes true. ARIA live regions need to exist in the DOM from page load to reliably fire announcements — dynamically mounting an element with aria-live does not guarantee the AT picks it up (tested failure mode on NVDA + Chrome). Fix: always render a wrapper `<div aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true'>` and conditionally render the content inside it. Bonus: this element also still lacks tabIndex, role=button, and onKeyDown (STRIKE 4 on keyboard accessibility).

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Worker card flip does not move keyboard focus to back panel
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 304-376: clicking the info button flips the card and sets the front panel to aria-hidden. But focus stays on the now-aria-hidden info button. A keyboard user is stranded. The back panel's Close button has tabIndex=0 when flipped, but nothing moves focus there. Fix: add a ref to the Close button, then after setIsFlipped(true) call closeButtonRef.current?.focus() inside a useEffect or callback. On close, return focus to the info button. Standard card-flip accessibility pattern.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: completionToast timer ref clobbers itself when multiple workers ship simultaneously
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 950-960: inside a forEach loop, each iteration overwrites `completionToastTimerRef.current` and `workerConfettiTimerRef.current`. If 3 workers ship at once, 3 staggered setTimeout calls are created but only the last timer ID is stored. On unmount, only the last timer gets cleared — the first two leak. Fix: store all timer IDs in an array ref (e.g. `completionTimersRef.current = []`) and clear all of them on cleanup.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: ErrorBoundary has no componentDidCatch — runtime errors vanish silently
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 7-20: ErrorBoundary has getDerivedStateFromError (shows fallback UI) but no componentDidCatch(error, info). In production, when something crashes, there is zero error reporting — no console output, no stack trace, no way to know what failed. For a live monitoring dashboard this is painful. Add componentDidCatch with at minimum console.error(error, info). Bonus: surface a 'Details' or 'Copy error' button in the fallback UI so the user can actually report the issue.

suggestions.title: NEW: Sofia's role is 'QA Analyst' in workers.json but constants only maps 'QA Engineer' — robot emoji fallback on dashboard
suggestions.description: The dashboard worker card shows Sofia with role 'QA Analyst'. ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS in constants.js only have 'QA Engineer' as a key. Result: getRoleEmoji('QA Analyst') returns the fallback '🤖', and Sofia's dashboard card avatar shows a robot. ROLE_COLORS also falls back to '#6b7280' (grey). The Office view is unaffected because CharacterAvatar checks workerId === 'sofia' directly. Fix: either update workers.json to use 'QA Engineer', or add 'QA Analyst' as an alias in the constants maps.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Footer tagline span is a ghost button — no role, no tabIndex, no keyboard handler
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1388: `<span className='footer-tagline' style={{cursor:'pointer'}} onClick={...}>`. Clicks cycle through quotes — but keyboard users cannot find or activate this. Add role='button' tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown (Enter and Space). Also: while you're touching this line, please remove the '1/9' pagination counter (STRIKE 5) right next to it.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: sync-status--paused card + polling-paused-pill both clickable but not keyboard-reachable
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 483: sync-status card has onClick=onResume when paused, no tabIndex, no role, no onKeyDown. Line 1174: polling-paused-pill span has onClick=onResume, same problem. Both let mouse users resume polling but lock out keyboard users. Two different interactive elements, same exact fix: tabIndex={pollingPaused ? 0 : -1} role='button' onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onResume() }}.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: stat-dispatch 'Scroll to workers' — pointer only, keyboard excluded
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 471-474: the Active count stat card's dispatch text has `style={{cursor:'pointer'}}` and onClick to scroll to the workers grid, but no tabIndex, no role='button', no onKeyDown. Add the standard interactive-span pattern.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: Activity log filter predicate duplicated — computed once for the count label, again to pass to ActivityLog
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1311-1323: an inline IIFE computes filtered.length using the type+worker filter predicates. Lines 1366-1375: the exact same filter is applied again to pass entries to <ActivityLog>. The same if/else chain is written twice. Extract: `const filteredEntries = activityLog.filter(typeFilter).filter(workerFilter)` before the JSX, then use `filteredEntries.length` for the count and `filteredEntries` for the component prop.

suggestions.title: NEW: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking defined inside useEffect — static objects recreated on every vibe change
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 986: `const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, ... }` and line 995: `const VIBE_LABELS = { 'after-hours': 'After Hours', ... }` — both defined inside the [teamVibe.key] useEffect. These are pure static constants with zero closure dependencies. They are recreated fresh on every vibe change. Move both to module scope alongside VIBE_COLORS and VIBE_RANK (which are already there, correctly).

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: getRelativeTime, formatDuration still living in App.jsx — four reviews of asking nicely
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 55-63 (getRelativeTime), 66-75 (formatFullDateTime), 244-252 (formatDuration): pure date utilities, no React, no state. Office.jsx has a duplicate inline relative-time calculation at lines 710-718. All of these belong in src/utils/time.js. Extract, export, import in both files. This is strike 4. If this hits strike 5, I'm filing it as a HIGH.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: isDimmed incorrectly dims workers with no task when a tag filter is active
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1264 and 1287: `isDimmed={selectedTag !== null && !getTaskTags(w.task).some(t => t.label === selectedTag)}`. When w.task is null/undefined, getTaskTags returns [] so .some() is false, so the worker gets dimmed. But a worker with no task is neutral — they haven't opted out of having a 'Design' or 'Bug' tag; they just have no task. Fix: `isDimmed={selectedTag !== null && !!w.task && !getTaskTags(w.task).some(t => t.label === selectedTag)}`.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: Office.css lines 91-95 are dead code — overridden completely by lines 97-115
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 use single-quoted attribute selectors (`[data-vibe='crushing']`). Lines 97-115 use double-quoted (`[data-vibe="crushing"]`). In CSS, single and double quotes in attribute selectors are equivalent — same specificity, same elements matched. Lines 97-115 come later and override all shared properties. The first block (91-95) is 100% dead code. The only thing the second block adds is the `--grid-line` custom property. Delete lines 91-95.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: Sparkline legend pills use array index as React key
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 417: `history.slice(-3).map((h, i) => <span key={i} ...>)`. Index keys cause incorrect reconciliation when the history array shifts — React will reuse existing DOM nodes instead of re-rendering with new values. Use `h.ts` (the timestamp from the vibe history entry object) as the key: `key={h.ts}`.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 6: Sub-pixel text in office floor is still unreadable on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 921: `fontSize: '0.38rem'` for the conference table last-huddle timestamp. Line 1480: `fontSize: '0.45rem'` for the lone-survivor label. These are 6-7px at standard viewport. They were illegible at desktop; on mobile they are literally invisible. Remove the inline text and convert to a tooltip (title attribute, already used elsewhere) or a tooltip on hover/tap. Or just delete the sub-pixel labels — they communicate nothing at that size.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 5: Footer tagline pagination counter (1/9) is ruining the secret easter egg
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1391: `{(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}`. The '1/9' counter turns a charming discoverable easter egg into a progress bar. It also reveals there are only 8 bonus quotes — the illusion of endless wit is shattered. The ✦ sparkle icon already signals interactivity. Remove the counter entirely. If discoverability is the concern, a subtle dot-row on hover is less intrusive.
